content.problemStatement:
Chef recently solved his first problem on CodeChef. The problem he solved has NNN test cases. He gets a score for his submission according to the following rules:



If Chef’s code passes all the NNN test cases, he gets 100100100 points.




If Chef’s code does not pass all the test cases, but passes all the **first** M  (M<N)M\;(M \lt N)M(M<N) test cases, he gets K  (K<100)K\;(K \lt 100)K(K<100) points.




If the conditions 111 and 222 are not satisfied, Chef does not get any points (i.e his score remains at 000 points).



You are given a binary array A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ of length NNN, where Ai=1A_i = 1Ai​=1 denotes Chef's code passed the ithi^{th}ith test case, Ai=0A_i = 0Ai​=0 denotes otherwise. You are also given the two integers M,KM, KM,K. Can you find how many points does Chef get?


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers N,M,KN, M, KN,M,K.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integer A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2,\dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single line the score of Chef.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains three space‑separated integers N, M and K (1 ≤ M < N, 1 ≤ K < 100). The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN where each Ai is either 0 or 1 indicating whether the i‑th test case was passed.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing Chef's score: 100 if all N test cases are passed, otherwise K if the first M test cases are all passed, otherwise 0.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: neither all 4 nor first 2 are passed → 0.
Test case 2: first 2 are passed, not all 3 → 50.
Test case 3: all 4 are passed → 100.
Test case 4: first 3 are not all passed → 0.
First 3 test cases are passed, but not all 5, so Chef gets K = 70 points.
All three test cases are passed, therefore Chef receives the full 100 points.

hidden.label: edge case – first M passed but not all
general case – fails within first M
general case – passes first M only

solution.approach: For each test case we scan the array once. First we check whether every element is 1; if so the answer is 100. If not, we check whether the first M elements are all 1; if so the answer is K. Otherwise the answer is 0. Both checks can be done in O(N) time, but we can stop early as soon as a 0 is found.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T·N) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input array.
